museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: June 2012: E3 2012 focuses on Wii U details and late-generation blockbusters.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for June 2012. Regional timing and UK retail context should be checked before verification.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: Five researched month markers or context notes.

items.title: E3 2012 focuses on Wii U details and late-generation blockbusters.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: The Last of Us is shown more clearly as a future PS3 prestige object.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: The industry is already looking past the machines it still depends on.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Kickstarter changes how players imagine funding and following games.
items.body: A year-level context marker included to frame the month without claiming a new event happened on a specific day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Wii U begins the next home-console cycle before Sony or Microsoft move.
items.body: A year-level context marker included to frame the month without claiming a new event happened on a specific day.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning
items.body: A bright RPG launches with big names and a complicated afterlife.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 2012.

items.title: Mass Effect 3
items.body: A trilogy ending turns player choice into a public argument.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2012.

items.title: Journey
items.body: Thatgamecompany makes online presence feel wordless and sacred.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2012.

items.title: The Walking Dead: Episode 1
items.body: Telltale makes episodic choice feel newly urgent.
items.sourceNote: Episode 1 is widely listed for April 2012.

items.title: Diablo III
items.body: Blizzard turns a long wait into server queues and auction-house arguments.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2012.

items.title: Darksiders II
items.body: A generous action RPG arrives as mid-tier console games start to feel fragile.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for August 2012.

items.title: Borderlands 2
items.body: Loot-shooter jokes and co-op routines become another long-term habit.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for September 2012.

items.title: Dishonored
items.body: Arkane turns assassination into a clockwork city of choices.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for October 2012.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PlayStation Vita launches in the West
items.body: Sony's handheld reaches Europe and North America into a market already changed by phones and 3DS recovery.
items.sourceNote: 2012 handheld references support February 2012 western launch context.

items.title: Wii U launches in the UK
items.body: Nintendo launches its tablet-controller home console in the UK and Europe on November 30.
items.sourceNote: Nintendo UK confirms November 30, 2012.

items.title: Kickstarter becomes visible
items.body: Double Fine Adventure turns crowdfunding into a serious game-development story.
items.sourceNote: Kickstarter project page and 2012 reporting support this context.

items.title: The old HD consoles remain dominant
items.body: PS3 and Xbox 360 still carry the year's biggest software despite Wii U's arrival.
items.sourceNote: 2012 release context supports this year-level reading.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Kickstarter makes players feel like patrons, backers and spectators of development.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: The Walking Dead turns episodic release dates into emotional appointments.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: Diablo III's always-online launch makes server access part of the launch story.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Miiverse arrives with Wii U as a strange, handwritten social layer.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the industry was asking who got to make games and who got to pay for them.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: Kickstarter gave players a seat too close to the kitchen; thrilling, intimate and occasionally uncomfortable.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: Journey and The Walking Dead made small or episodic games feel emotionally central.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: Wii U arrived as an idea people wanted to understand, but the old consoles kept shouting over it.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
